title: Product name: Decision Explorer AI
question: What is the public name of the product for launch?
rationale: Name the public product 'Decision Explorer AI' - which unifies the name with the viewer and signals the tool is AI-native (Claude-driven) - while keeping the /decision-tree command, the skills/decision-tree install path, and the project folder unchanged, since renaming those is risky and user-facing in a different way. The viewer title/brand/footer, generate.py embedded copy, SKILL.md, README, netlify.toml and _project.json all now say Decision Explorer AI.

history.reason: Added 'AI' so the public name signals the tool is AI-native / Claude-driven; same low-risk scope as the original rename (command, install path and folder untouched).

options.label: Rebrand the product to 'Decision Explorer AI'

tradeoffs.note: the 'AI' suffix signals the tool is AI-native / Claude-driven, matching how it's actually used
viewer title, brand, footer and docs all updated together, so the app and product share one name
command, install path and folder untouched; only displayed name and docs change
matches the viewer (already titled Decision Explorer), so the user-facing app and the product share one name
command and paths untouched, so nothing breaks; only docs, repo slug and the displayed name change
repo slug changes to Decision-Explorer, but GitHub redirects the old Decision-Tree URL
leaves two names - 'Decision Tree' the product vs 'Decision Explorer' the viewer - which reads as a mismatch
no repo rename or doc churn at all

options.label: Rename everything including the /decision-tree command and folder

tradeoffs.note: fully unified - even the command would read /decision-explorer
rewrites the install path, every hardcoded path and the session's working directory; high chance of breakage right before launch
